CSEP (Critical Skills Employment Permit).tagline: Fast-track employment permit for in-demand roles (€38k+ salary threshold applies).

steps.title: Confirm your role is on the Critical Skills list
steps.description: Check that your job title and employer qualify. CSEP is for occupations on Ireland's Critical Skills Occupations List with minimum salary thresholds.

steps.title: Employer applies for CSEP
steps.description: Your employer submits the Critical Skills Employment Permit application to DETE (Department of Enterprise, Trade and Employment) before you travel.

steps.title: Gather passport, contract & qualifications
steps.description: Prepare certified copies of degree/professional certs, signed contract, and passport valid 6+ months.

steps.title: Apply for Irish entry visa (if required)
steps.description: Non-visa-required nationals can travel once permit is approved. Others apply for D visa at Irish embassy/consulate.

steps.title: Register with immigration (IRP / GNIB)
steps.description: Within 90 days of arrival, book registration for Irish Residence Permit (Stamp 1 or as directed). Bring permit letter, passport, proof of address.

steps.title: Apply for PPS number
steps.description: Required for employment and tax. Book at your local Intreo/office with proof of address and employment.

steps.title: Open Irish bank account
steps.description: Bring IRP card, PPS letter, employment contract, and proof of address.

steps.title: Understand Stamp 4 pathway
steps.description: After 21 months on CSEP (with same employer conditions), you may be eligible to apply for Stamp 4 — verify current rules on irishimmigration.ie.

steps.title: Track permit renewal dates
steps.description: CSEP is tied to employment. If you change jobs, a new permit may be required before starting new role.

tips: Salary must meet the minimum for your occupation category — confirm current thresholds on enterprise.gov.ie.
Keep digital copies of all immigration correspondence.
Join Expal Community forums for CSEP holders in your city.

General Work Permit.tagline: Standard employment permit when your role is not on the Critical Skills list.

steps.title: Labour Market Needs Test (LMNT)
steps.description: Employer must usually show the role was advertised to EU/EEA candidates first. Confirm your employer completed LMNT unless exempt.

steps.title: Employer submits General Employment Permit
steps.description: Application goes to DETE. Processing times vary — plan several months before intended start date.

steps.title: Check salary & contract minimums
steps.description: General permits have minimum salary requirements (typically €30,000+ depending on role). Contract must meet permit conditions.

steps.title: Entry visa (if applicable)
steps.description: Once permit approved, apply for entry visa at Irish embassy if your nationality requires one.

steps.title: Register for IRP after arrival
steps.description: Book immigration registration within 90 days. Bring permit approval, passport, employer letter, proof of address.

steps.title: PPS number & tax registration
steps.description: Apply for PPS to start employment legally. Employer registers you for PAYE.

steps.title: Bank account & payroll setup
steps.description: Irish bank account needed for salary. Some banks accept appointment letter before IRP arrives.

steps.title: Plan permit renewal early
steps.description: General permits are usually issued for up to 2 years. Start renewal 3–6 months before expiry.

steps.title: Explore long-term residency options
steps.description: After 5 years on valid permits you may qualify for long-term residency — track all stamps and absences.

tips: Changing employer usually requires a new permit application.
Keep payslips and tax documents from day one.
Use Journey → Timeline for admin deadlines.

EU Passport / EU Citizen.tagline: EU/EEA/Swiss citizens can live and work in Ireland without an employment permit.

steps.title: Travel with valid EU/EEA/Swiss passport or national ID
steps.description: No employment permit required. You have freedom of movement to live and work in Ireland.

steps.title: Find accommodation & move
steps.description: You can arrive and start job hunting or begin employment immediately — no permit wait.

steps.title: Register residence if staying 3+ months
steps.description: EU citizens staying over 3 months should register — requirements depend on circumstances (employment, study, etc.).

steps.title: Apply for PPS number
steps.description: Needed for employment and accessing services. Book appointment with required ID and proof of address.

steps.title: Open bank account
steps.description: Passport/national ID plus proof of address. Employment contract helps if employed.

steps.title: Register for healthcare
steps.description: EU citizens may use EHIC initially; apply for Irish medical card or private insurance depending on income.

steps.title: Understand tax residency
steps.description: Living in Ireland 183+ days/year usually makes you tax resident. Register with Revenue.

steps.title: Optional: apply for Irish citizenship later
steps.description: After 5 years legal residence you may apply for naturalisation — different rules than non-EU permits.

tips: No employment permit means faster job changes — still need PPS and contract.
Brexit note: UK citizens now follow separate rules — not covered by EU freedom of movement.
Update your visa type in Profile if you switch status.

Work Permit.tagline: General work-authorisation pathway for your destination country.

steps.title: Confirm permit type with employer
steps.description: Ask HR which permit category applies to your role and nationality.

steps.title: Gather identity & employment documents
steps.description: Passport, contract, qualifications, and any police clearance required.

steps.title: Register locally after arrival
steps.description: Complete immigration and address registration within required deadlines.

tips: Always verify rules on official government immigration sites.
